data.title: Morning News Wrap-Up: Thursday's Biggest Stock Market Stories!
data.description:
Investors seeking the biggest stock market stories today are in the right place. TipRanks has collected a list of the top stock market stories traders need to know about on Thursday.

  • Caterpillar (CAT) stock rallied after the construction equipment company beat Q4 2025 estimates.
  • Lululemon Athletica (LULU) stock was down after the athletic wear company faced criticism for its poor quality control.
  • Dow (DOW) stock was down alongside plans to cut 4,500 jobs.
  • Meta (META) stock was on the rise after the social media company posted its latest earnings report.
  • ServiceNow (NOW) stock slipped despite news of a new share buyback program.
  • The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) was down alongside the latest data from the Department of Labor.
  • Microsoft (MSFT) stock took a beating after the tech giant failed to impress investors with its latest earnings report.
  • Comcast (CMCSA) stock soared alongside the entertainment company's most recent earnings report.
  • Nvidia (NVDA) stock dipped despite praise from a top analyst.
  • TEN Holdings (XHLD) stock climbed on the company's attendance at a special conference.
  • Mastercard (MA) stock was up after the financial firm posted Q4 earnings that were above estimates.
  • The S&P 500 (SPX) was down on a lackluster trade balance update from the Commerce Department.
  • Serina Therapeutics (SER) stock rocketed on the FDA's clearance for the company's investigational Parkinson's treatment.
  • MP Materials (MP) stock slipped alongside an upgrade on mineral trade project prices from the Trump administration.
  • General Motors (GM) stock got a boost after the automaker said it would scale back production in Canada.
  • Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and Qualcomm (QCOM) shares fell despite reports that the two would adopt SOCAMM2 memory for AI products.
  • SoundHound AI (SOUN) stock took a beating even after the AI company announced an expanded partnership with Five Guys.
  • Amazon (AMZN) stock dropped on reports that its gaming head would depart the company.
  • Carvana (CVNA) stock jumped after analysts defended the used-car retailer from a short report.
  • Blackstone (BX) stock fell even after the investment company posted strong earnings.

Read more of the top stock market stories at TipRanks.
data.author: William White

data.title: Eli Lilly (LLY) Inks $1.12 Billion Deal with 'Gene-Editing' Firm Seamless
data.description:
U.S. pharmaceutical giant Eli Lilly (LLY) has signed an agreement worth up to $1.12 billion with Seamless Therapeutics, a privately held German startup company that specializes in gene editing.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Eli Lilly said it plans to work with Seamless to develop and commercialize ‌treatments for hearing loss using the biotech firm's gene-editing platform. The deal gives Eli Lilly access to Seamless' technology that engineers enzymes to correct gene mutations that lead to hearing loss in people.

These enzymes, called "programmable recombinases," are designed to make changes to a person's DNA that help prevent hearing loss. Eli Lilly said it will oversee ‌preclinical testing through to commercialization of the gene-editing platform and hearing loss treatment.

Eli Lilly's Diverse Pipeline

Eli Lilly's $1.12 billion investment in Seamless includes an upfront payment, funding for research and development, as well as future payments upon completing certain development and commercial milestones, said the two companies in a news release.

While Eli Lilly gets a lot of attention for its Zepbound weight-loss medication, the company has a diverse pipeline of new medicines in development, including related to ⁠genetic treatments for several diseases. In 2025, Eli Lilly spent $1.3 billion to buy Verve Therapeutics and acquire its gene-editing ‍therapies that help reduce ⁠high cholesterol in people with heart disease.

Is LLY Stock a Buy?

The stock of Eli Lilly has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 20 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 18 Buy and two Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average LLY price target of $1,191.37 implies 12.08% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: SoFi Technologies (SOFI) Is About to Report Q4 Earnings Tomorrow. Here Is What to Expect
data.description:
Fintech company SoFi Technologies (SOFI) is scheduled to announce its results for the fourth quarter of 2025 on Friday, January 30. The stock has surged about 54% in 2025, fueled by strong financials and rapid growth in its customer base. Wall Street expects SoFi to report earnings of $0.12 per share, down 58.6% from the same period last year. Meanwhile, revenue is projected to come in at $977.42 million, compared with $734 million in the prior-year quarter.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Investors will be watching for updates on loan growth, credit quality, and guidance when the company reports its earnings.

Analysts' Views Ahead of SOFI's Q4 Earnings

Ahead of the Q4 print, Bank of America analyst Mihir Bhatia resumed coverage with a Sell rating and a price target of $20.50. The 5-star analyst views SoFi's recent $1.5 billion capital raise as a "modest positive," as it gives the company added flexibility to invest in growth. That said, the analyst remains cautious on the stock, citing limited upside at current valuation levels.

Meanwhile, Barclays analyst Terry Ma increased the price target for SOFI stock to $28 from $23 and reaffirmed a Hold rating. The 4-star analyst sees further upside in select consumer finance stocks in 2026, supported by what he expects to be a benign credit environment. Ma also believes improving credit conditions could help drive loan growth and expects the mortgage origination market to recover in 2026, providing an additional tailwind for the sector.

Loan trends will be a key focus this quarter, especially as analysts look for signs of a pickup in mortgage and personal loan activity. As seen in the chart below, personal loans remain SoFi's largest lending category, while home loan originations are starting to pick up after a long slowdown.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Microsoft Stock (MSFT) Gets Price Target Cuts After Latest Earnings
data.description:
Several analysts are lowering their price targets on Microsoft's (MSFT) stock after the technology giant's latest financial results.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Analysts at Scotiabank (BNS) lowered their price target on MSFT stock to $600 from $650. At the same time, TD Cowen (TD) took its price target on Microsoft's shares down to $610 from $625. However, both firms maintained a Buy rating on Microsoft's stock following the company's print.

Analysts at JPMorgan Chase (JPM) also weighed in, trimming their price target on MSFT stock to $550 from $575, while maintaining a Buy-equivalent overweight rating on the shares. The lowered price targets come after Microsoft's results showed slowing cloud growth and rising capital expenditures on artificial intelligence (AI).

Microsoft's Difficult Results

Microsoft managed to beat Wall Street forecasts on its top and bottom line results for what was its fiscal second quarter. However, Microsoft announced that revenue from its cloud computing unit grew 39%, down from 40% growth in the previous quarter. Wall Street was expecting 39.4% growth from the cloud unit.

Microsoft's capital expenditures and finance leases in the most recent quarter, which are mostly comprised of spending on AI, came to $37.5 billion, up 66% from a year earlier. That was more spending than the $34.31 billion that Wall Street had anticipated. The result is that MSFT stock is down 12% on Jan. 29, a day after the print.

Is MSFT Stock a Buy?

Microsoft's stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 36 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 35 Buy and one Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average price target on MSFT stock of $604.13 implies 42.77% upside from current levels. These ratings could change further after the company's financial results.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: S&P 500 Stumbles as U.S. Trade Deficit Nearly Doubles to $56 Billion
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX) is falling on Thursday after the Commerce Department provided a disappointing trade balance update.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In November, the U.S. trade deficit nearly doubled to $56.8 billion from $29.2 billion, above the consensus estimate of $44 billion. The data was delayed due to last year's government shutdown.

During the month, imports increased by 5% to $348.9 billion, while exports fell by 3.6% to $292.1 billion.

Rising Imports Pressure Economic Growth

Rising imports are bad news for gross domestic product (GDP) because they increase the trade deficit, meaning more spending flows overseas rather than contributing to domestic growth. The growth in imports was led by pharmaceuticals, computers, computer accessories, and semiconductors.

Despite the month-over-month rise, November's trade deficit remains relatively low compared to the past three years.

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Amazon Stock (AMZN) Slides on Gaming Head's Departure
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN) stock was down on Thursday alongside news that the head of its gaming division is leaving the company. According to these reports, Amazon Game Studios leader Christoph Hartmann will leave the company as it switches focus away from developing games for PC and consoles. Instead, Amazon will reportedly focus more on its Luna game streaming platform.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Hartmann's reported departure comes at the same time that Amazon has revealed massive job cuts. The company said it will cut 16,000 jobs, though it didn't clarify if its gaming division was included. Amazon Game Studio already faced layoffs late last year, and the company has announced the end of life for its New World MMORPG.

All of this shows that Amazon wasn't cut out for the video game market. Its internal projects couldn't attract enough players to warrant continuation, and the company failed to establish itself as a competent PC game storefront, despite its strong focus on e-commerce. Amazon attempted to be the next Steam, the leading PC game storefront, but failed to event make a dent in the market.

Amazon Stock Movement Today

Amazon stock was down 2.19% on Thursday but was still up 2.99% year-to-date. The company's shares have also rallied 3.57% over the past 12 months.

Amazon stock movement today saw some 17 million shares change hands, compared to a three-month daily average trading volume of about 34.77 million units.
data.author: William White

data.title: Carvana Stock (CVNA) Rallies after JPMorgan Defends It from Short Seller
data.description:
Shares of online used-car retailer Carvana (CVNA) are rallying today after Wall Street analysts came to the company's defense following a short report from Gotham City Research. Notably, that report had knocked the stock down by about 14% the day before, as it raised questions about Carvana's financial practices. However, five-star JPMorgan (JPM) analyst Rajat Gupta, who has a Buy rating and a $510 price target on Carvana, said that the short report misunderstood key financial numbers.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

According to Gupta, the report mixed long-term, cumulative figures with annual results, which led to incorrect conclusions. In addition, he said he was surprised by how sharp the stock's drop was, especially given what he described as fairly straightforward math related to asset-backed securities and well-known challenges from 2022 and 2023 loans. He also said the report wrongly described how service income from DriveTime flows through Carvana's loan-related businesses.

Unsurprisingly, Carvana strongly rejected the accusations as well. In a statement on Wednesday, a company spokesperson said that the report was inaccurate and intentionally misleading. The company stated that all related-party transactions are fully disclosed in its financial statements. Carvana also reminded investors that it plans to release its 2025 earnings on February 18, 2026, which suggests that upcoming results should help address investor concerns.

Is CVNA Stock a Good Buy?

Overall, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on CVNA stock based on 16 Buys, two Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average CVNA price target of $790.88 per share implies 87.6% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: NVIDIA Links Quantum Computing's Future to Its GPU Platform
data.description:
NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) published a policy-focused post on Wednesday that offers a clear signal about how the next phase of quantum development may unfold in the U.S. Rather than spotlighting narrow lab milestones, the company framed quantum computing as part of a broader system that already includes AI and high-performance computing. As a result, the message carries more weight for investors tracking long-term platform strategy than for readers focused on near-term scientific progress.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For years, quantum computing has been difficult to place in a market context. Hardware progress remains early, timelines are uncertain, and few companies show a clear path to commercial scale. However, NVIDIA's post presents a different view, where quantum value comes from how it connects to existing computing systems rather than from standalone breakthroughs.

At the center of the post is a call for Congress to reauthorize the National Quantum Initiative, a program launched in 2018 to coordinate U.S. quantum research across national labs, universities, and industry. According to NVIDIA, the original strategy now needs an update because AI has become essential to making quantum systems useful at scale. The company argues that quantum and AI should no longer be treated as separate efforts but instead combined into what it describes as a new class of scientific machines.

As NVIDIA wrote, "AI and quantum computing are no longer just distinct tools, but the foundational elements of a new class of supercomputers." These systems would blend GPUs, CPUs, and quantum processors into a single environment designed to solve complex scientific problems.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: SOUN Stock Drops despite Five Guys Partnership Expansion
data.description:
SoundHound AI (SOUN) stock dipped despite news that the company expanded its partnership with Five Guys. The deal boosts SOUN's presence in enterprise AI, but investors remain cautious, keeping the stock under pressure. Following the announcement, SOUN fell 2% yesterday and is down another 4.5% so far today.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, SoundHound AI specializes in voice recognition and natural language processing, offering AI-driven solutions across industries. Meanwhile, Five Guys is a fast-casual burger chain known with hundreds of locations across the U.S. and internationally.

More Details on the Partnership

The renewal shows the strength of the partnership, as SoundHound's AI-powered ordering agents have already handled over a million customer interactions for Five Guys. Under the renewed deal, Five Guys will let franchisees expand use of the AI technology.

Using SoundHound's voice AI, the chain can take 100% of orders, even during busy times, reducing missed orders and letting customers order quickly and accurately. For staff, this means fewer interruptions, allowing them to focus on in-store service, food prep, and delivering a great guest experience.

What This Means for SoundHound

The expanded partnership with Five Guys strengthens SoundHound's position in the enterprise AI market and highlights the growing demand for its voice AI technology. The system has already handled customer interactions, enabling faster and more accurate ordering and demonstrating its value in real-world settings.

The deal also paves the way for wider adoption across franchise locations, potentially boosting revenue and visibility for SoundHound—even as investors watch the stock closely for short-term swings.

Is SOUN a Good Stock to Buy?

According to TipRanks, SOUN stock has received a Moderate Buy consensus rating, with five Buys and two Holds assigned in the last three months. The average SoundHound stock price target is $16.60, suggesting a potential upside of 83.53% from the current level.
data.author: Kirti Tak
